verdict_reasoning: SRC is an N-myristoylated non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane and endomembranes — not extracellularly accessible at baseline. However, ecto-SRC / surface-displayed active SRC has been documented on tumor cells as part of the broader cancer ecto-kinome, qualifying as cell-state-induced outer-leaflet display. No germline/developmental narrow-lineage surface restriction; not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented PM minority pool with own extracellular anchor (inner-leaflet anchored at baseline); no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering described. The cancer ecto-display is the only contextual surface story.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Strength of ecto-SRC evidence on tumor cells is limited; could plausibly be `no`/`inner_leaflet_anchored` if cancer surface display is not credited.
